campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held their regular meeting on Tuesday, February 3rd, 2026. All council members were present, including Mayor Fai, Vice Mayor Scazola, and council members Lopez, Hines, and Bby. The meeting opened with the Pledge of Allegiance led by Leslie Fabila from the Public Works Department.
keyTopics: Regular City Council meeting convened
All five council members present
Public Works Department participation

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council met to decide how to distribute grant money to local organizations and events. They approved funding for specific organizations, set aside money for competitive grants that groups can apply for, and made some updates to the rules for how community events can get funding and use city streets.
keyTopics: Approved specific grant recipients and dollar amounts for secured funding
Set funding budget for the Competitive Event Grant Program for the fiscal year
Updated and clarified rules for Community Event and Street Closure Grants

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — April 22, 2025
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met to approve routine financial and administrative items, including the treasurer's report and meeting minutes. The council adopted new lighting performance standards for the city, accepted a property map for 705 Vista Grande Avenue, and discussed how the city handles sponsoring special events and creating stormwater assessment districts.
keyTopics: New lighting performance standards adopted
705 Vista Grande Avenue property officially accepted
City funding allocation approved
Special events sponsorship and city involvement discussed
Stormwater assessment district formation process reviewed

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 3, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on March 3, 2026 to approve records from their previous meeting and finalize the diversity and inclusion commission's plans for the year. The council also heard from a resident named Nimi Kumar and discussed how they format and present meeting minutes to the public.
keyTopics: Approved February 17 meeting minutes
2026 Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Commission work plan approval
Public comment from Nimi Kumar
Changes to how meeting minutes are formatted and shared

san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This was a San José City Council meeting held on March 25, 2026. The agenda focused on providing accessibility options for residents to watch the meeting through multiple platforms including cable TV, online streaming, and phone dial-in, with live translations available in over 50 languages.
keyTopics: Multiple ways to watch the meeting (cable, YouTube, Zoom, phone)
Live translation services in 50+ languages available
Meeting accessible via phone dial-in for those without internet
Online streaming through official city channels

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council's zoning hearing that was supposed to happen on March 25, 2026 has been cancelled. If you had plans to attend or submit concerns about a zoning issue, the next opportunity will be on April 8, 2026.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
Rescheduled to April 8, 2026
Administrative zoning decisions postponed

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community development projects in Sunnyvale. The commission approved meeting minutes from the previous month and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to support housing and human services programs.
keyTopics: Federal grant applications for affordable housing projects
Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding decisions
Human services program funding requests
Approval of February 2026 meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on helping local businesses grow and succeed. They discussed a program to help businesses get proper licenses, plans to promote shopping at local stores, and ways the city can partner with private companies to sponsor events and support new shops and startups.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program to help shops get properly licensed
Outreach to shopping centers to boost local business participation
Summer campaign encouraging residents to shop local
City event sponsorships through partnerships with private companies
Financial incentives and support for retail stores, small businesses, and startups

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on March 24, 2026 to address several administrative matters and hear from the public on two main issues: a program to remove overgrown weeds around the city in partnership with the county, and how federal housing grant money should be spent in the coming year.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program for 2026 - removing overgrown weeds on properties
Federal HUD housing grant funding plans for fiscal year 2026/27
Multiple agenda items postponed or reconsidered

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — February 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: I don't have access to the actual meeting minutes or agenda details from the February 17, 2026 Milpitas City Council meeting. To provide you with an accurate summary of what was discussed, I would need the full meeting transcript, agenda items, or official minutes document.

Please provide the meeting minutes or agenda
